title: salix triandra subsp. amygdalina
intro: Salix triandra subsp. amygdalina, also known as the almond-leaved willow or almond-leaved sallow, is a subspecies of the Salix triandra plant that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix triandra subsp. amygdalina include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and skin conditions like eczema. Herbal preparations of Salix triandra subsp. amygdalina can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
